interfaces.name: FermentFamily
interfaces.signature: enum FermentFamily: String, Codable, CaseIterable, Sendable { case krautKimchi, sourdough, kombucha, genericCustom }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: concrete ferment set for v1 plus generic lower-confidence fallback.

interfaces.name: BatchLocationTag
interfaces.signature: enum BatchLocationTag: String, Codable, Sendable { case kitchen, cellar, counter, pantry, other }
interfaces.notes: Optional fixed vocabulary location tag for future learned-profile grouping.

interfaces.name: TasteCheckOutcome
interfaces.signature: enum TasteCheckOutcome: String, Codable, Sendable { case tooEarly, close, inTheZone, pastIdeal }
interfaces.notes: Constrained taste outcomes that the engine can reason over.

interfaces.name: ConfidenceLevel
interfaces.signature: enum ConfidenceLevel: String, Codable, Sendable { case high, moderate, low }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: never shown as a percentage.

interfaces.name: BatchLifecycleState
interfaces.signature: enum BatchLifecycleState: String, Codable, Sendable { case active, completed, archived }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED.

interfaces.name: NotificationCapability
interfaces.signature: enum NotificationCapability: String, Codable, Sendable { case scheduled, manualWatch, denied, unsupportedDueToCapacity }
interfaces.notes: Derived capability shown in UI and used by scheduler.

interfaces.name: BatchRecord
interfaces.signature: struct BatchRecord: Identifiable, Codable, Sendable { let id: UUID; var name: String; var family: FermentFamily; var locationTag: BatchLocationTag?; var createdAt: Date; var lifecycleState: BatchLifecycleState; var archivedReason: String?; var completedAt: Date? }
interfaces.notes: Plain value type used everywhere outside SwiftData mapping.

interfaces.name: BatchEventKind
interfaces.signature: enum BatchEventKind: Sendable { case created(startTemperatureF: Double?); case ambientTemperatureLogged(tempF: Double); case tasteCheckLogged(outcome: TasteCheckOutcome); case manualWatchSet(Bool); case completed; case archived(reason: String); case notificationEligibilityChanged(NotificationCapability) }
interfaces.notes: Append-only fact model; payloads captured in associated values.

interfaces.name: BatchEventRecord
interfaces.signature: struct BatchEventRecord: Identifiable, Codable, Sendable { let id: UUID; let batchID: UUID; let occurredAt: Date; let kind: BatchEventKind }
interfaces.notes: Immutable timestamped event; engine input and history source of truth.

interfaces.name: DerivedBatchStatus
interfaces.signature: struct DerivedBatchStatus: Sendable { let batchID: UUID; let engineVersion: Int; let statePhrase: String; let reasoning: String; let confidence: ConfidenceLevel; let notificationCapability: NotificationCapability; let likelyWindowStart: Date?; let likelyWindowEnd: Date?; let isOverdue: Bool; let lastObservationAt: Date?; let sparklineTrendSummary: String }
interfaces.notes: Pure engine output consumed by UI and scheduler; not authoritative persisted truth.

interfaces.name: EntitlementState
interfaces.signature: enum EntitlementState: Sendable { case free; case premium(activeProductID: String, expirationDate: Date?) }
interfaces.notes: Single source of truth for gating decisions.

interfaces.name: PlanCapabilities
interfaces.signature: struct PlanCapabilities: Sendable { let maxActiveBatches: Int?; let editableHistoryDays: Int?; let allowsUnlimitedStoredHistory: Bool }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED.

interfaces.name: deriveBatchStatus
interfaces.signature: func deriveBatchStatus(batch: BatchRecord, events: [BatchEventRecord], now: Date) -> DerivedBatchStatus
interfaces.notes: Pure pacing engine entry point. No Date() calls inside.

interfaces.name: rankActiveBatches
interfaces.signature: func rankActiveBatches(_ batches: [Batch], statuses: [UUID: DerivedStatus], now: Date) -> [Batch]
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: isolates home-screen urgency ordering from re-rank animation diffing (diff on id+rank position only).

interfaces.name: BatchRepository
interfaces.signature: final class BatchRepository { func fetchAllBatches() async throws -> [Batch]; func fetchEvents(for batchID: UUID) async throws -> [ObservationEvent]; func createBatch(_ batch: Batch, initialEvents: [ObservationEvent]) async throws; func appendEvent(_ event: ObservationEvent) async throws; func updateLifecycleState(batchID: UUID, state: BatchLifecycleState, reason: ArchiveReason?) async throws; func deleteBatch(id: UUID) async throws }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: concrete class (not protocol), matching ios_architecture_review's no-repository-ceremony call. Backed internally by a ModelActor-conforming executor so writes happen off the main actor.

interfaces.name: HistoryRepository
interfaces.signature: final class HistoryRepository { func fetchCompletedBatches(offset: Int, limit: Int) async throws -> [Batch]; func fetchArchivedBatches(offset: Int, limit: Int) async throws -> [Batch] }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: explicit pagination params so nobody fetch-all-then-slices in memory.

interfaces.name: NotificationScheduling
interfaces.signature: protocol NotificationScheduling { func refreshNotification(for batch: BatchRecord, status: DerivedBatchStatus) async; func removeNotification(forBatchID batchID: UUID) async; func currentAuthorizationState() async -> NotificationCapability }
interfaces.notes: One pending local notification per batch UUID; generic durable content only.

interfaces.name: EntitlementProviding
interfaces.signature: protocol EntitlementProviding { func currentEntitlement() async -> EntitlementState; func capabilities(for entitlement: EntitlementState) -> PlanCapabilities; func beginTransactionUpdatesListener() async; func purchasePremiumMonthly() async throws; func purchasePremiumAnnual() async throws; func restorePurchases() async throws }
interfaces.notes: StoreKit 2 wrapper; views do not talk to StoreKit directly.

interfaces.name: BatchStore
interfaces.signature: @Observable @MainActor final class BatchStore { private(set) var activeBatches: [Batch]; private(set) var derivedStatuses: [UUID: DerivedStatus]; var rankedActiveBatches: [Batch] { get }; var canCreateAdditionalActiveBatch: Bool { get }; func loadActiveBatches(now: Date) async throws; func createBatch(name: String, fermentFamily: FermentFamily, locationTag: LocationTag?, startDate: Date, initialTemperatureCelsius: Double?, now: Date) async throws -> Batch; func logTemperature(batchID: UUID, celsius: Double, at: Date) async throws; func logTasteCheck(batchID: UUID, outcome: TasteOutcome, at: Date) async throws; func manualOverrideCheck(batchID: UUID, at: Date) async throws; func completeBatch(batchID: UUID, at: Date) async throws; func deleteBatch(batchID: UUID) async throws; func recomputeStale(now: Date) async }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: persist-then-derive, no optimistic/rollback UI. Repository write is kicked off on submit and runs concurrently with the already-locked sheet-dismiss/recalculation transition (masking budget, not hope); only falls back to the existing rare-persistence-error/retry state if the write outlasts that transition. Swipe actions and detail buttons call these same methods, never duplicate logic. recomputeStale reassigns notification eligibility via CapEnforcement.notificationEligibleBatchIDs and reconciles NotificationScheduler.scheduleOrUpdate/cancelNotification as part of the same authoritative pass — not just staleness decay.

interfaces.name: HistoryStore
interfaces.signature: @Observable @MainActor final class HistoryStore { func loadCompleted(offset: Int, limit: Int) async throws -> [Batch]; func loadArchived(offset: Int, limit: Int) async throws -> [Batch] }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: thin wrapper over HistoryRepository's paginated fetches.

interfaces.name: CreateBatchInput
interfaces.signature: struct CreateBatchInput: Sendable { var name: String; var family: FermentFamily; var locationTag: BatchLocationTag?; var startAt: Date; var startingTemperatureF: Double? }
interfaces.notes: Shared creation payload across form UI and batch creation logic.

interfaces.name: LogObservationInput
interfaces.signature: struct LogObservationInput: Sendable { let batchID: UUID; let occurredAt: Date; let payload: ObservationPayload }
interfaces.notes: Bottom-sheet submission contract.

interfaces.name: ObservationPayload
interfaces.signature: enum ObservationPayload: Sendable { case ambientTemperature(tempF: Double); case tasteCheck(TasteCheckOutcome) }
interfaces.notes: Only two observation types allowed in v1.

interfaces.name: createBatch
interfaces.signature: func createBatch(from input: CreateBatchInput, now: Date) throws -> (BatchRecord, [BatchEventRecord])
interfaces.notes: Deterministic constructor for new batch and initial event set.

interfaces.name: makeObservationEvent
interfaces.signature: func makeObservationEvent(from input: LogObservationInput) -> BatchEventRecord
interfaces.notes: Centralizes event creation so UI and persistence do not invent variants.

interfaces.name: AppFailure
interfaces.signature: enum AppFailure: Error, Sendable { case persistenceFailed; case invalidInput(String); case notificationsDenied; case purchaseFailed; case restoreFailed }
interfaces.notes: Codex's broader narrow-error-vocabulary type; overlaps with BatchStoreError — round 2 should confirm whether both are needed or one subsumes the other.

interfaces.name: ReleaseChecklistProbe
interfaces.signature: protocol ReleaseChecklistProbe { func verifyNotificationSchedulingRealDevice() async -> Bool; func verifyTimezoneChangeBehavior() async -> Bool }
interfaces.notes: OPEN: Claude argues this should be removed entirely from the machine contract (nothing can genuinely automate it, so a protocol here is a stub; belongs in a plain manual release-checklist doc instead). Codex's round-2 message still includes it, written before seeing this argument — needs his direct response in round 3.

interfaces.name: Batch
interfaces.signature: struct Batch: Identifiable, Equatable, Sendable { let id: UUID; var name: String; var fermentFamily: FermentFamily; var locationTag: LocationTag?; var startDate: Date; var initialTemperatureCelsius: Double?; var lifecycleState: BatchLifecycleState; var archivedReason: ArchiveReason?; let createdAt: Date; var completedAt: Date? }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED canonical cross-lane batch value type.

interfaces.name: LocationTag
interfaces.signature: enum LocationTag: String, Codable, CaseIterable, Sendable { case kitchen, cellar, counter, pantry, other }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: semantic label only, never device location; backfills learned-profile precondition from detailed_discussion.

interfaces.name: TasteOutcome
interfaces.signature: enum TasteOutcome: String, Codable, CaseIterable, Sendable { case tooEarly, close, inTheZone, pastIdeal }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: constrained taste outcomes feeding the pacing engine.

interfaces.name: ObservationEventKind
interfaces.signature: enum ObservationEventKind: Equatable, Sendable { case created(startTemperatureCelsius: Double?); case temperatureLogged(celsius: Double, isBackdated: Bool); case tasteChecked(outcome: TasteOutcome); case manualWatchSet(Bool); case notificationEligibilityChanged(eligible: Bool, reason: NotificationIneligibilityReason?); case archived(reason: ArchiveReason); case completed }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: append-only fact model; persisted truth is facts, never a cached countdown.

interfaces.name: ObservationEvent
interfaces.signature: struct ObservationEvent: Identifiable, Equatable, Sendable { let id: UUID; let batchID: UUID; let at: Date; let kind: ObservationEventKind }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: absolute Date only, never calendar-day math.

interfaces.name: NotificationIneligibilityReason
interfaces.signature: enum NotificationIneligibilityReason: String, Codable, Sendable { case permissionDenied, capacityExceeded, batchArchived, batchCompleted, manualWatchRequested }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: capacityExceeded covers the 64-pending-notification ceiling fallback from ios_architecture_review.

interfaces.name: ArchiveReason
interfaces.signature: enum ArchiveReason: String, Codable, Sendable { case planLimitDowngrade, userInitiated }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED.

interfaces.name: StatePhrase
interfaces.signature: enum StatePhrase: String, Codable, Sendable { case checkToday, hold, overdue, manualWatch }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: fixed vocabulary from design_handoff, UI never invents freeform phrases.

interfaces.name: DerivedStatus
interfaces.signature: struct DerivedStatus: Equatable, Sendable { let batchID: UUID; let engineVersion: Int; let statePhrase: StatePhrase; let reasoningSentence: String; let confidence: ConfidenceLevel; let confidenceExplanation: String; let windowStart: Date?; let windowEnd: Date?; let isOverdue: Bool; let lastObservationAt: Date?; let notificationEligible: Bool; let notificationIneligibilityReason: NotificationIneligibilityReason?; let sparklineTrendSummary: String; let computedAt: Date }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: pure engine output, never hand-edited by UI or persistence.

interfaces.name: PacingEngine.deriveStatus
interfaces.signature: enum PacingEngine { static let currentEngineVersion: Int; static func deriveStatus(batch: Batch, events: [ObservationEvent], now: Date) -> DerivedStatus }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: pure function, now always injected, no Date() inside. Core acceptance-test target for DST/timezone/off-schedule guarantees.

interfaces.name: CapEnforcement
interfaces.signature: enum CapEnforcement { static func canCreateActiveBatch(currentActiveCount: Int, tier: SubscriptionTier) -> Bool; static func batchesToArchiveOnDowngrade(activeBatches: [Batch], historyCutoff: Date, tier: SubscriptionTier) -> [UUID]; static func notificationEligibleBatchIDs(rankedActiveBatches: [Batch], safetyCeiling: Int) -> Set<UUID> }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: pure, testable, never deletes data. notificationEligibleBatchIDs drives capacity reshuffling as batches complete/are created, adopted unopposed across rounds 2-3.

interfaces.name: AppForegroundCoordinator
interfaces.signature: @Observable @MainActor final class AppForegroundCoordinator { private(set) var notificationAuthorizationStatus: UNAuthorizationStatus; func handleDidBecomeActive(now: Date) async }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: single owner of foreground re-entry (permission recheck, stale/eligibility recompute, entitlement refresh); confirmed load-bearing by Codex round 2.

interfaces.name: BatchStoreError
interfaces.signature: enum BatchStoreError: Error, Sendable { case persistenceFailed(underlying: Error); case batchNotFound(UUID); case activeCapReached }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED as the only shared store-facing error type; no generic app-wide AppFailure bus. activeCapReached should rarely surface since canCreateAdditionalActiveBatch is checked pre-sheet.

interfaces.name: NotificationScheduler
interfaces.signature: actor NotificationScheduler { func scheduleOrUpdate(for batch: Batch, status: DerivedStatus) async; func cancelNotification(for batchID: UUID) async; func currentAuthorizationStatus() async -> UNAuthorizationStatus; func requestAuthorization() async throws -> Bool }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: upsert-by-identifier via add(); explicit cancel only on eligibility exit. Body copy generic/durable only, never confidence/reasoning text.

interfaces.name: EntitlementManager
interfaces.signature: @Observable @MainActor final class EntitlementManager { private(set) var tier: SubscriptionTier; var capabilities: PlanCapabilities { get }; func refreshEntitlements() async; func beginTransactionUpdatesListener() async; func purchase(_ product: Product) async throws; func restorePurchases() async throws }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: trusts Transaction.currentEntitlements only; purchase/restore failures mapped locally from StoreKit's own error types, no shared AppFailure.

interfaces.name: SubscriptionTier
interfaces.signature: enum SubscriptionTier: String, Sendable { case free, premium }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: premium means unlimited stored data/history, never unlimited simultaneous scheduled reminders.

interfaces.name: SparklineGenerator.generate
interfaces.signature: struct SparklinePoint: Sendable { let at: Date; let celsius: Double }; enum SparklineTrend: String, Sendable { case rising, falling, stable, insufficientData }; struct SparklineData: Sendable { let points: [SparklinePoint]; let trend: SparklineTrend; let accessibilitySummary: String }; enum SparklineGenerator { static func generate(from temperatureEvents: [ObservationEvent]) -> SparklineData }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: one function feeds both Canvas drawing and VoiceOver string so a visual tweak can't silently desync from the spoken summary.

interfaces.name: BatchModel
interfaces.signature: @Model final class BatchModel { /* private to Persistence module; mirrors Batch, relates to ObservationEventModel; VersionedSchema + SchemaMigrationPlan applied here */ }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: never imported outside the Persistence module.

interfaces.name: ObservationEventModel
interfaces.signature: @Model final class ObservationEventModel { /* private to Persistence module; mirrors ObservationEvent, kind stored as encoded payload; append-only, never mutated post-insert */ }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED.
